trajectory.description: Observation: Fresh start, no files yet. Analysis: Need to train PPO on 10 HumanoidBench h1hand tasks. Key constraints: (1) no info_keywords='success' for 8/10 tasks, (2) use cumulative reward >= success_bar for success, (3) per-task models since obs dims differ. Strategy: Train per-task PPO with VecNormalize, use SubprocVecEnv with spawn method. Start with a quick env test, then train all 10 tasks. Decision: Write train.py and predict.py, do a quick test first, then start training.

trajectory.description: Observation: Environment test passed. obs shape (151,), action shape (61,), info keys confirmed. train.py and predict.py are already written. Analysis: Need to start training. With 32 envs and 2M steps per task, estimated ~125 sec/task = ~21 min for all 10 tasks. Let me start training with 2M steps per task to get a baseline. Decision: Run training with TOTAL_TIMESTEPS=2000000 for all 10 tasks, then submit eval.

Observation: Training ran for 2068s and exited with code 120 (killed). The workspace shows logs/iter_2/walk/metrics.jsonl exists, meaning walk task was trained. But other tasks may not have checkpoints. Analysis: The sequential training of 10 tasks with 32 envs and 2M steps each is too slow. Need to reduce timesteps and envs. Also need to check what checkpoints exist before submitting eval. Decision: Check existing checkpoints, then submit eval with whatever exists. Also reduce training to 1M steps with 16 envs for faster iteration.

trajectory.description: Observation: Walk task trained for 2M steps in 34 min, mean_reward ~12 (normalized). Only walk checkpoint exists. Analysis: The normalized reward of 12 doesn't tell us actual performance. Need to submit eval to see real scores. Also need to train all 10 tasks - with 9.4h remaining, I should use 500k-1M steps per task with fewer envs. The sequential training approach is too slow. Decision: Submit eval with current walk checkpoint (other tasks score 0), then start training all 10 tasks with 1M steps each using 16 envs. Also check if walk checkpoint actually has good performance.

trajectory.description: Observation: Walk checkpoint directory is empty - training was killed before saving. The eval scored 0 for all tasks. Analysis: The training ran for 2068s (34 min) for walk alone with 2M steps. It was killed before completing all 10 tasks. The checkpoint wasn't saved because the process was killed. I need to: (1) Add checkpoint saving during training (not just at end), (2) Reduce steps per task to fit in time budget, (3) Use more envs for faster training. With 9.4h remaining and 26 iterations, I should train all 10 tasks. Each task at 1M steps with 32 envs should take ~17 min. 10 tasks = 170 min = 2.8h. Decision: Rewrite train.py with checkpoint callbacks saving every 200k steps, use 32 envs, 1M steps per task. Start training immediately.
